task_completion.description: The YDBGui web interface is open and running.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open displaying the YDBGui interface.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the ^LAB(61) global.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The global explorer or tree view in YDBGui shows ^LAB(61) entered in the search/navigation bar or selected in the global list.

task_completion.description: At least one entry under ^LAB(61) is expanded to reveal its zero-node.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tree view shows at least one child node of ^LAB(61) (e.g., ^LAB(61, 1)) expanded, specifically showing the '0' (zero-node) sub-node.

task_completion.description: At least three entries under ^LAB(61) are expanded to reveal their zero-nodes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tree view shows a minimum of three distinct child nodes of ^LAB(61) expanded, with their respective '0' (zero-node) sub-nodes visible.

task_completion.description: Common specimen names are visible in the zero-node data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The data values corresponding to the expanded zero-nodes explicitly display standard specimen names such as 'BLOOD', 'URINE', 'SERUM', 'PLASMA', or 'CEREBROSPINAL FLUID'.

integrity.description: The agent used the YDBGui web interface rather than terminal commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: The data is displayed within the YDBGui web browser interface, not in a terminal emulator or MUMPS command line prompt.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected specimen names into a text editor or echo them.
integrity.visual_evidence: The specimen names appear strictly within the YDBGui global explorer data fields, not in a text editor like nano, vim, or a basic terminal echo.

integrity.description: The results appear to come from genuine database interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: The specimen names are formatted as standard VistA zero-node strings (e.g., delimited by carets '^') within the YDBGui tree structure.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target global: ^LAB(61). Target file name: TOPOGRAPHY FIELD. Expected specimen names in the zero-node data include BLOOD, URINE, SERUM, PLASMA, and CEREBROSPINAL FLUID. The interface used must be the YDBGui web interface.
